headline: Action Chain System
orientation: The Action Chain system replaces hard‑coded Rust pipelines with data‑driven YAML chains that build hierarchical pyramid nodes. It defines a runtime composed of six Rust modules, a rich chain schema, variable resolution, multiple step execution modes, resume support, and error handling, while exposing model tier configuration and a workflow for custom variants. Default chains exist for conversation, code, and document pipelines; future work adds vine, delta, meta chains, Wire sync, and a UI editor.

topics.current: Chains are defined in YAML + Markdown prompts; steps specify model, temperature, error handling, and optional node persistence. Supports resume from exact failure point and variant forks per pyramid.
topics.name: Purpose & Benefits
topics.summary: Data‑driven YAML chains replace ~8k‑line hard‑coded Rust pipelines, enabling recompilation‑free edits, per‑step model control, resume, variants, and observability.

topics.current: Execution starts with `run_chain_build(slug)`, resolves chain ID, loads YAML, validates, builds contexts, then iterates steps per mode, persisting outputs and nodes via an async writer drain.
topics.name: Runtime Architecture
topics.summary: Six Rust modules (chain_engine, chain_resolve, chain_dispatch, chain_executor, chain_loader, chain_registry) implement validation, reference resolution, LLM/mechanical dispatch, execution loop, loading, and SQLite mapping.

topics.current: Validation enforces schema_version=1, non‑empty id/name, content_type ∈ {conversation,code,document}, unique step names, primitive validity, mechanical step must have rust_function, LLM step must have instruction, and on_error syntax.
topics.name: Chain Definition Schema
topics.summary: Top‑level fields (schema_version=1, id, name, content_type, defaults, steps) describe a chain; defaults give model tier, temperature, on_error; steps list ordered primitives with per‑step overrides.

topics.current: Built‑in scalars include $chunks, $slug, $content_type; loop variables ($item, $index) and pair variables ($pair.left, etc.) are available. Undefined required refs cause runtime errors; string interpolation produces stringified values.
topics.name: Variable Resolution
topics.summary: Two resolvers: `$variable.path` in YAML inputs resolves against ChainContext; `{{variable}}` in Markdown prompts resolves from the step’s resolved input map.

topics.current: Modes are selected by flags (`for_each`, `pair_adjacent`, `recursive_pair`, `mechanical`). Each mode handles iteration, resume checks, and node creation accordingly.
topics.name: Step Execution Modes
topics.summary: A step runs in exactly one mode: forEach (loop over array), pair_adjacent (pair nodes at a depth), recursive_pair (repeat pairing to apex), single (once), or mechanical (Rust function).

topics.current: Accumulator config (`field`, `init`, `max_chars`, `trim_to`, `trim_side`) updates `$running_context` after each LLM output; on resume, prior outputs are replayed to reconstruct state.
topics.name: Sequential Accumulation
topics.summary: For forEach steps with `sequential: true`, an accumulator carries a running summary across iterations, truncating per configured limits.

topics.current: Resume state can be Complete, StaleStep, or Missing. Retry uses exponential backoff (2‑s, 4‑s, 8‑s). LLM JSON parse failures auto‑retry once at temperature 0.1 before invoking the step’s on_error.
topics.name: Resume & Error Handling
topics.summary: Dual‑check resume (step output + node) guarantees correctness; five error strategies (`abort`, `skip`, `retry(N)`, `carry_left`, `carry_up`) are applied per‑step or via defaults.

topics.current: Default tier is `mid` (config.primary_model). Direct model overrides (e.g., "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4") bypass tier mapping. Temperature defaults to 0.3, overridden per step.
topics.name: Model Configuration
topics.summary: Four tiers (low, mid, high, max) map to OpenRouter model slugs; precedence order: step `model` > defaults `model` > step `model_tier` > defaults `model_tier`.

topics.current: Creating a variant copies a default YAML, changes `id`, edits steps, and assigns via the DB. `chain_file` is a cached hint; on startup the directory is rescanned.
topics.name: Custom Chains & Variants
topics.summary: Chains live under `{data_dir}/chains/` (defaults and variants). Assignment to a pyramid slug is stored in SQLite `pyramid_chain_assignments`.

topics.current: Conversation chain includes forward_pass, reverse_pass, combine_l0, l1_pairing, thread_clustering, thread_narratives, upper_layers. Code and document chains differ in L0 extraction and L1 grouping but share clustering and upper layers.
topics.name: Default Chains
topics.summary: Three shipped chains (conversation, code, document) each consist of ~6‑7 steps using primitives such as compress, synthesize, classify, and pair modes to build L0‑L2 nodes and a final apex.

topics.current: Vine will nest existing pyramids; Delta will rebuild only affected nodes; Meta will emit META‑* overlay nodes. Wire enforces schema_version compatibility. Frontend phases: manager list, drag‑and‑drop editor, agent‑hand‑off schema export/import.
topics.name: Future Roadmap
topics.summary: Wave 2 adds Vine (meta‑pyramid), Delta (incremental rebuild), Meta (metadata nodes). Wave 3 introduces Wire sync for distributed chain sharing and a UI chain manager/editor with schema import/export.
